Okay, so rather than making a bunch of small videos, I'm just going to knock all five of these things out in about three minutes. So here we go. So, LTB, lifetime value. One of the things that you're going to have to take into account when you're thinking about the 12-week transformation challenge is that these people are going to have significantly higher lifetime value. And that's because the likelihood that someone who spends $2,000 can afford staying at $299, $399, $499 a month is fairly high. And your ability to service them, get them amazing results because they pay you so much, is also higher. So they're going to have a better experience. So these are people who are more likely to spend more money, got better results. Selecting that they stay a lot longer is really high. So these are people who really take lots of care of because they can become the quarters of your business. Okay, next one, value ladder. What's the other main reason to introduce a higher ticket program? Because people want to know what the next thing is, right? After people have done the bootcamp for a while, they usually fall off because it's just not a very high value service, right? Same thing with CrossFit. And I have nothing against it. Obviously, everyone has their raving group of fans and they're like, why can't I have more of those? And it's just because there's a lot of deliberate effort that goes into it. But you usually need another ascension, just something up that's higher that's a little bit more personal. And 20% of buyers are going to be hyperactive. And so you need to allow them to give you more money, right? Which is why value ladders exist. So as long as you're with me in the marketing world, etc., you need to have a value ladder. So this is the next national rung for most people. And it's really, really important because what that does is that's going to relate down to these two in a second. So just pocket that. Long term stability. The reason I say it's actually all these are really related, which is why it's one video. Okay. Value ladder concept is important because every time a percentage of people buy the next thing that increases the entire value of the entire chain below it. So let's illustrate. So let's say you've got $100 offer, $1000 offer and $10,000 offer just for math sake. And you get 100 people to buy your $100 offer, which is 10K. So 100 people buy $100 offer, which is 10K. Okay. Now because you have a value ladder, this is why it's important, you get 20% of those people to buy your next thing, right? And your next thing is $1000. Okay. And then your next thing is 10K, right? And you get 20% of these people, which is 4 to buy your 10K. Which is 40K. So what I'm trying to demonstrate here is the importance of these, this value ladder concept. Okay. It's usually isn't 80, 20. It's fractal. But you can see how dramatic, like this entire funnel made 70 grand, but 60 of that 70, 6 7ths of that was from the two upsells afterwards from only 24 customers. Like let that sink in because when I understood this with my business, like everything changed. So I want you to understand this with your business because there are people in your business right now who would love to give you more money for more service, but you don't have that. So you want to make that available to them. Okay. Now here's where it gets even way cooler. Okay. So if you're making $10,000 from 100 sales, right? There you go. Scenario one. Pay up to $100 to acquire customer before breaking even. Okay. With scenario two, let's say you just had room one and room two. Okay.
